mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales indicates robust consumer demand, which translates into higher corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon. This is a fundamental demand-side driver of equity performance.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward returns for S&P 500 consistently show a negative correlation with the month-over-month growth rate of RSAFS over any 3-year rolling window, or if the Rank IC is below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate, specifically a rise of 0.5 percentage points or more over three months, signals a deteriorating labor market that will likely lead to reduced consumer spending and corporate profits, causing negative S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive or flat in more than 40% of instances where UNRATE rises by 0.5pp or more over three months, or if the average return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) is high and the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) is simultaneously rising, it indicates aggressive monetary tightening to combat inflation, which typically leads to higher discount rates and reduced corporate valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL growth > 0.3% MoM AND FEDFUNDS change > 0.1% MoM
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of periods where both CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS meet the specified trigger conditions, or if the average return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During periods of strong economic expansion, robust housing starts (HOUST) indicate strong investment and consumer confidence, which provides a positive sentiment boost to equities, leading to short-term positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: If, during periods identified as late-cycle expansion, HOUST exceeding 1500 does not lead to positive 5-day S&P 500 returns more than 55% of the time, or if the average return is not statistically significant (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in industrial production (INDPRO), specifically a drop of 1% or more month-over-month, signals a contraction in manufacturing and overall supply-side activity, which negatively impacts corporate earnings and leads to lower S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 35% of instances where INDPRO drops by 1% or more MoM, or if the average return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) during such periods.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent job creation, as measured by non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS), indicates a healthy labor market and strong consumer base, which underpins corporate revenue growth and supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward returns for S&P 500 consistently show a negative correlation with the month-over-month growth rate of PAYEMS over any 3-year rolling window, or if the Rank IC is below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When GDP growth (GDPC1) is strong and retail sales (RSAFS) are also accelerating, it signifies robust aggregate demand, boosting investor confidence and leading to short-term positive S&P 500 returns as market participants price in strong economic momentum.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 QoQ growth > 3.0% annualized AND RSAFS MoM growth > 0.5%
failure_condition: If S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 45% of periods where both GDPC1 and RSAFS meet the specified growth conditions, or if the average return is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During periods of low inflation, a stable or declining Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) signals an accommodative monetary policy, which reduces the cost of capital for businesses and supports higher equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: If, in a low inflation regime (CPIAUCSL growth < 0.2% MoM), a stable or declining FEDFUNDS does not lead to positive 60-day S&P 500 returns more than 60% of the time, or if the average return is not statistically significant (t-test p > 0.05).
